(2) On an application under this rule, the court may direct that steps already taken to bring the application form to the attention of the respondent by an alternative method or at an alternative place is good service.


(3) A direction under this rule must specify—


### Power of the court to dispense with service of the application E+W


6.20. —(1) The court may dispense with service of the application where it is impracticable to serve the application by any method provided for by this Part.


(2) An application for an order to dispense with service may be made at any time and must be supported by evidence.


(3) The court may require the applicant to attend when it decides the application.


### Notification of failure of [ F37 postal ] service by the court E+W


6.21. Where—


the court will send notification to the applicant that the application has been returned.


Textual Amendments


F37 Word in rule 6.21 heading inserted (6.4.2022) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2022 (S.I. 2022/44) , rules 1(3)(a) , 19 (with rule 29 ); S.I. 2022/283 , reg. 2


### [ F38 Notification of failure of email service by the court E+W


6.21A. Where –


the court officer will send notification to the applicant that the application was undeliverable. ]


Textual Amendments


F38 Rule 6.21A inserted (6.4.2022) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2022 (S.I. 2022/44) , rules 1(3)(a) , 20 (with rule 29 ); S.I. 2022/283 , reg. 2


### Notice of non-service by bailiff E+W


6.22. Where—


the court officer will send notification to the applicant.


### CHAPTER 3 E+W SERVICE OF DOCUMENTS OTHER THAN AN APPLICATION FOR A MATRIMONIAL ORDER OR CIVIL PARTNERSHIP ORDER IN THE UNITED KINGDOM


### Method of service E+W


6.23. A document may be served by any of the following methods—


(Rule 6.35 provides for the court to permit service by an alternative method or at an alternative place.)
